articles.title: General Mills selling China ice-cream shops to investors
articles.summary: General Mills is selling its Häagen-Dazs ice cream shops in mainland China to an investor group including Chinese tea brand Ningji. This deal grants the buyers exclusive rights to sell Häagen-Dazs in shops and gifting businesses across mainland China, while General Mills will continue retail and food service sales. The move comes as foreign businesses adjust operations in China due to stagnating consumer confidence and slowing economic growth, following similar joint ventures by Starbucks and Burger King.

articles.title: Starbucks chief joins Evercore fireside chat, live webcast June 9
articles.summary: Starbucks Corporation announced that its chairman and CEO, Brian Niccol, will participate in a keynote fireside chat at the 6th Annual Evercore Consumer and Retail Conference on Tuesday, June 9th, 2026. The event is scheduled for 11:40 a.m. Eastern Time and will be webcast live on the company's Investor Relations website. This participation highlights Starbucks' engagement with the investor community and its visibility in the consumer and retail sector.

articles.title: Starbucks Corp. stock underperforms Tuesday when compared to competitors
articles.summary: Starbucks Corp. (SBUX) stock fell 1.04% on Tuesday, closing at $95.51, despite a positive trading session for the broader market. Both the S&P 500 Index (SPX) and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) saw gains. This marked the fourth consecutive day of losses for Starbucks' stock.

articles.title: Target’s June sale: up to 45% off plus free Starbucks treat
articles.summary: Target announced its "Target Circle Deal Days" sale from June 23–26, offering up to 45% off thousands of items in various categories, with early access for Target Circle 360 members on June 22. The event includes special promotions like 40% off back-to-school essentials and a free Starbucks coffeehouse treat for Target Circle members on June 23. These deals aim to help families save money during summer and back-to-school preparation, reinforcing Target's focus on value and loyalty programs.

articles.title: Peapack Gladstone Financial Corp Has $10.88 Million Holdings in Starbucks Corporation $SBUX
articles.summary: Peapack Gladstone Financial Corp reduced its stake in Starbucks Corporation by 10.2% in the fourth quarter, selling 14,686 shares but still holding 129,190 shares valued at approximately $10.88 million. Despite this reduction, institutional ownership remains significant, with many large managers like Vanguard and Envestnet Asset Management increasing their positions. Starbucks recently reported stronger-than-expected quarterly results and holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts with an average price target of $107.48.

articles.title: MUFG Securities EMEA plc Purchases 24,858 Shares of Starbucks Corporation $SBUX
articles.summary: MUFG Securities EMEA plc significantly increased its stake in Starbucks Corporation by purchasing 24,858 additional shares in Q4, bringing their total holdings to 46,615 shares valued at approximately $3.93 million. This comes as institutional interest in Starbucks remains high, with 72.29% of the company owned by institutions, and analysts maintaining a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average price target of $107.48. Starbucks recently reported solid quarterly results, surpassing earnings and revenue expectations.

articles.title: Starbucks to Take AI Usage into Account in Tech Workers’ Bonuses
articles.summary: Starbucks is linking a quarter of its tech workers' bonuses to their use of artificial intelligence, aiming to improve efficiency and justify its AI investments. The company is tracking AI adoption as part of its "Back to Starbucks" strategy, with software developers expected to use an AI assistant multiple times weekly. This initiative reflects a broader corporate trend of integrating and measuring AI usage within workflows to boost productivity and customer experience.

articles.title: 5th Circ. Judge Asks Starbucks Why Co. Doesn't Want Unions
articles.summary: A Fifth Circuit judge questioned Starbucks' anti-union stance during an appeal of a National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) ruling. The NLRB had previously found that Starbucks suppressed workers' rights in response to a 2021 organizing effort in upstate New York. The judge's inquiry focused on understanding the company's core philosophy regarding unions.

articles.title: Has Starbucks Reached the Turning Point in Its Turnaround Story?
articles.summary: Starbucks appears to be reaching a significant inflection point in its turnaround efforts, with its "Back to Starbucks" strategy showing progress in revenue and earnings growth for the first time in over two years. The company reported improved comparable-store sales in the U.S. and positive growth across major international markets, signaling a move beyond stabilization into sustainable growth. Despite competition from Dutch Bros and McDonald's, Starbucks' focus on better service, loyalty benefits, and the coffeehouse experience is helping restore its operational excellence and brand appeal.

articles.title: Arcadia Investment Management Corp MI Sells 21,387 Shares of Starbucks Corporation $SBUX
articles.summary: Arcadia Investment Management Corp MI reduced its stake in Starbucks Corporation by 19.0% in the fourth quarter, selling 21,387 shares and leaving it with 91,415 shares valued at approximately $7.7 million. Despite this, Starbucks reported stronger-than-expected quarterly results with an EPS of $0.50 and revenue of $9.53 billion, both exceeding analyst estimates. The company also affirmed its FY 2026 EPS guidance and is seeing mixed sentiment due to improving afternoon traffic but also concerns regarding a retired AI inventory tool and international issues in South Korea.

articles.title: 'Familiarity does not equal immunity': Outrage over Starbucks Korea's 'Tank Day' campaign refuses to quell
articles.summary: Starbucks Korea is facing a severe backlash and significant financial losses due to its "Tank Day" campaign, which offensively coincided with May 18, the anniversary of the 1980 Gwangju Uprising. The campaign, promoting "Tank Series" tumblers, was widely perceived as disrespectful to a day of national trauma, leading to boycotts, condemnations from government officials and delivery unions, and a public apology from parent company Shinsegae. Brand experts emphasize that the incident highlights a failure by Starbucks to genuinely understand Korean culture, suggesting that while the situation is not irreversible, regaining trust requires demonstrating a deeper understanding of the mistake's cultural impact.

articles.title: Starbucks’ AI Disaster and Sam Altman’s U-Turn Prove That AI Still Needs Humans
articles.summary: Starbucks recently fired its AI inventory management system after it proved disastrous due to incorrect product recognition and counting errors, highlighting the need for robust underlying infrastructure and human oversight in AI implementation. This incident, along with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's revised stance emphasizing the essential human element in most work, suggests that AI will likely augment rather than fully replace human jobs, especially in complex systems. The article assesses Starbucks' stock (SBUX) performance and analyst ratings, noting its recent quarterly growth and future prospects.

articles.title: Fideuram Asset Management Ireland dac Purchases New Position in Starbucks Corporation $SBUX
articles.summary: Fideuram Asset Management Ireland dac has acquired a new stake in Starbucks Corporation, purchasing 15,869 shares valued at approximately $1,336,000 in the fourth quarter. Other institutional investors also bought new positions in Starbucks, while insider activity showed sales from EVP Sara Kelly and CEO Brady Brewer. The article also provides a roundup of recent Starbucks news, price performance, dividend information, and analyst ratings.
